brief: Application fails to connect to database in staging but works in development. No error message — connection silently falls back to localhost.

root_cause.description: The config loader reads process.env.DATABASE_URL, but the staging deployment template sets DATABASE_URI. The variable is never defined in staging, so process.env.DATABASE_URL returns undefined and the fallback default of 'localhost:5432' silently applies, causing all staging connections to target the wrong host with no log warning.
root_cause.mechanism: process.env.DATABASE_URL returns undefined (env var is DATABASE_URI), fallback default of localhost:5432 silently applies

required_findings.description: Agent must identify the database configuration module as the failure area
Agent must identify the DATABASE_URL vs DATABASE_URI environment variable name mismatch
Agent must identify that the silent fallback to localhost masks the misconfiguration — no error is raised
Agent must identify database.ts line 12 as the exact location of the defective env var read with silent fallback

anti_patterns.description: The connection reaches localhost:5432, not the staging database host; the issue is a misconfigured variable name, not a firewall or network rule
The connection is silently redirected to localhost:5432 where no Postgres is running; credential validity is never tested
Container networking is not the cause; the environment variable name mismatch means the correct host is never read from the environment

notes: A score-4 investigation will also note that adding a startup assertion (throwing if DATABASE_URL is undefined rather than silently defaulting) would make this class of misconfiguration immediately visible in logs. The deployment template is correct — DATABASE_URI is the canonical name in the infrastructure team's naming convention; it is the application config loader that uses the wrong name.
